title: Executioner
content:
{{Otheruses|Executioner}} {{CharHub|Executioner|Skurge}} {{Character |image = Skurgetheexecutionerprofile.png |real name = Skurge |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Executioner{{Ref|TR}}}} |species = [[Asgardian]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|ASG1}} |gender = Male |DOD = [[2017|Late 2017]]{{Ref|TR}}{{Ref|MSTMCUAOT}} |title = Guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]] (formerly)
Executioner of [[Asgard]] (formerly) |affilitation = [[Einherjar]] (formerly) |movie = ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'' |book = ''[[Avengers: Infinity War: The Heroes' Journey]]'' |actor = [[Karl Urban]] |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|I just want a chance to prove myself.|Skurge to [[Hela]]|Thor: Ragnarok}} '''Skurge''' was a former [[Einherjar]] warrior who was tasked with becoming the guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]] by [[Loki]] following the banishment of [[Heimdall]]. When Loki was revealed to have been ruling [[Asgard]] in secret, Skurge was demoted and made the janitor; however, he was later recruited by [[Hela]] to become her personal '''Executioner'''. However, Skurge's disillusion and inner conflict of his new position upon seeing her brutality against her own people and the threat she posed to the [[Nine Realms]] ultimately made him turn against the Goddess of Death. During his final moments, Skurge fought Hela's entire army of the [[Berserkers]] and then allowed all of his people to survive the [[Destruction of Asgard]] at the cost of his own life. These selfless actions finally made Skurge a hero of Asgard. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== ====[[Battle of Vanaheim]]==== {{Quote|Who are you?"
"Don't you remember? I'm Skurge, we [[Battle of Vanaheim|fought]] together on [[Vanaheim]]."
"Right...|[[Thor]] and Skurge|Thor: Ragnarok}} Born in [[Asgard]] to a stonemason father, Skurge had one ambition throughout his young life, to prove himself as an [[Asgardian]] warrior worthy of being remembered. Skurge trained to eventually become a soldier within [[Einherjar]], prepared to sacrifice his own life for the protection of the throne and his fellow Asgardians. Within Einherjar, Skurge fought alongside [[Thor]], [[Sif]], and the [[Warriors Three]] during the [[Battle of Vanaheim]] against the [[Marauders]] during the [[War of the Nine Realms]]. Despite fighting alongside one another in [[Vanaheim]], Thor had no idea who Skurge was, despite Skurge holding the memory close to his heart.{{Ref|TR}} ===Bifrost Guardian=== ====Replacing Heimdall==== {{Quote|[[Heimdall]] was an idiot. This job should have made him rich. Now the job ain't easy, but it does have its benefits. The [[Bifrost Bridge|Bifrost]] gives me access to everything the [[Nine Realms]] has to offer, I mean it's all mine for the taking.|Skurge|Thor: Ragnarok}} [[File:Thor-ragnarok-karl-urban.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge is named the Guardian of the Bifrost]] Skurge was finally given his own long-awaited opportunity to prove himself when [[Loki]], who was currently still disguised as [[Odin]], was ruling [[Asgard]], banished [[Heimdall]], claiming it to be due to neglecting his duty of protecting Asgard. As a result of Heimdall's banishment, Loki then made Skurge the newest guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]] and was given possession of [[Hofund]]. He was also assigned to announce [[Thor]]'s arrival whenever Thor had returned to Asgard since the disguised Loki granted Thor independence allowing him to travel between all the [[Nine Realms]] as he pleased to do so. [[File:Skurge's Shake Weight.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge shows off his collection of items]] In reality, this was secretly to give Loki a warning that Thor was on his way back so Loki would have time to switch back to Odin's strict personality. While Skurge resided in [[Himinbjorg]], he took advantage of the new role to impress women as well as to travel to all locations, obtaining many items to add to his collection, including a pair of {{WPS|M-16 Rifle}}s from [[Earth]] which he named [[Des and Troy]], with Skurge claiming that when put together, they would destroy.{{Ref|TR}} ====Thor's Return==== [[File:SkurgeShowingOff.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge showing off to his beautiful dates]] {{Quote|Well, well. Look who decided to pop in. Thanks for scaring away my company and drenching my workplace in brains.|Skurge to [[Thor]]|Thor: Ragnarok}} One day, while still on duty, Skurge was again personally entertaining two [[Asgardian]] ladies inside of [[Himinbjorg]] as he began boasting about how [[Heimdall]] was a fool for not taking advantage of having complete access to all of the [[Nine Realms]]. Skurge then showed off his collection of stolen items to both of the girls, including a {{WPS|Shake Weight}} before showing them [[Des and Troy]] which particularly impressed them. [[File:Skurge's Skurge.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge is covered with [[Fire Dragon]] blood]] However, Skurge's showing offended when [[Thor]], who was currently under attack in [[Muspelheim]], called for transport. Because he was distracted, Skurge missed the initial call until one of his companions pointed it out. When Skurge finally activated the [[Bifrost Bridge]], Thor came through followed by the head of the Bifrost-decapitated [[Fire Dragon]] that had been chasing him, which sprayed its blood everywhere, causing his completely horrified companions to leave in utter disgust. [[File:Thor and Skurge.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge tells [[Thor]] of [[Heimdall]]'s disappearance]] With both of his beautiful companions having run away, the irritated Skurge confronted Thor over scaring them away and drenching Himinbjorg in Dragon blood. When Thor questioned who Skurge was, he reminded him of how they had previously fought together during the [[Battle of Vanaheim]] where they had battled against all the vast armies of [[Marauders]], although Thor had clearly still struggled to remember Skurge as Thor then asked of [[Heimdall]]'s whereabouts. [[File:SCIDBFWHDIH.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge watches [[Thor]] fly back into [[Asgard]]]] Skurge then explained about Heimdall's banishment and that he was now the Guardian of the Bifrost ever since he had gone. Thor then flew away to see [[Odin]], ignoring the fact that Skurge was meant to announce any arrivals to Odin first, unaware they were actually made by Loki in order to prepare him if Thor had arrived. Skurge was then forced to awkwardly run across the [[Rainbow Bridge]] to announce his arrival, hoping to arrive before the king just before Thor did. [[File:LokiSurvivalRevealed-ThorRagnarok.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge fails to announce [[Thor]]'s arrival in time]] Skurge eventually made his way to announce Thor, only to be too late, as Odin was revealed as [[Loki]], which made him furious. Irritated that Skurge had failed to give him enough warning, Loki reminded him of how Skurge had been given just one job, to announce Thor's arrival, and Skurge had failed to do even that. Skurge then watched as Thor then used [[Mjølnir]] to force Loki to confess to Odin's current location on [[Earth]] as they both went to bring him back home.{{Ref|TR}} ===Hela's Return=== ====Recruited by Hela==== [[File:Skurge & Fandral.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge and [[Fandral]] witnessing [[Hela]]'s arrival]] {{Quote|I'm just the janitor."
"You look like a smart boy with good survival instincts.|Skurge and [[Hela]]|Thor: Ragnarok}} With [[Loki]]'s deception finally revealed, Skurge was removed from his position as guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]] and was given the task of cleaning up all the [[Fire Dragon]]'s blood which was covering almost the entire floor and walls of [[Himinbjorg]]. Under the supervision of [[Volstagg]] and [[Fandral]], Skurge was busy mopping the floor when the Goddess of Death, [[Hela]], had returned to [[Asgard]], much to everyone's surprise. [[File:Thor Ragnarok 109.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge chooses to follow [[Hela]]'s new rule]] Volstagg demanded to know what happened to [[Thor]] and [[Loki]] who they had expected to come through the Bifrost, however, Hela had refused to answer and responded by launching her [[Necroswords]] at the warriors, [[Siege of Asgard|killing them both]] in seconds while Skurge looked on in horror. As Hela walked over to him, Skurge claimed to only be the janitor, impressing Hela with his survival skills which convinced her to recruit him to help her in finally overthrowing all of Asgard. [[File:Thor Ragnarok 55.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge chooses to follow [[Hela]]'s new rule]] With no other possible choice that would result in his survival having seen the massacre of two members of the [[Warriors Three]], Skurge reluctantly got back onto his feet and followed behind Hela, leaving the bodies of Volstagg and Fandral behind. Skurge then followed Hela as she walked down the [[Rainbow Bridge]] with the intention of taking over all of Asgard and claiming the [[Royal Palace of Valaskjalf]] as her own, leaving [[Hofund]] behind still inside Himinbjorg. [[File:HelaExecutioner-HogunsLastStand.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge overlooks [[Hela]]'s murderous rampage]] They were approached by the entire [[Einherjar]] army who had come to attempt to stop Hela from achieving her goals. However, Skurge then rejoined Hela after she had single-handedly [[Siege of Asgard|slaughtered]] all of the Einherjar with considerable ease, killing the soldiers and bringing down the [[Asgardian Skiff]]s with her own Necroswords. The horrified Skurge was then left to walk around the field of corpses while Hela delighted in her complete and utter victory in the battle. [[File:Skurge-SadFace-HogunsDeath.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge watches as [[Hela]] finally kills [[Hogun]]]] Skurge was left completely helpless as he then watched Hela brutally slaughter [[Hogun]] who remained as the last soldier standing at the end of the horrifyingly brutal [[Siege of Asgard]] before Hela decided to return to her [[Royal Palace of Valaskjalf|Palace]]. During all this utter violence, Skurge was still completely horrified and ashamed of the ruthlessness that he was standing by of, but at the same time, he wanted to keep himself safe so he silently continued with Hela towards the throne room.{{Ref|TR}} ====The Executioner==== [[File:HelaSkurge-AsgardsTrueHistory.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge is shown the true history of [[Asgard]]]] {{Quote|Tell me about yourself, Skurge."
"Well, my dad was a stonemason. My mom was..."
"Right, yeah, I'll just stop you there.|[[Hela]] and Skurge|Thor: Ragnarok}} Skurge and [[Hela]] then made it to the [[Royal Palace of Valaskjalf]], where Hela then started to talk about how everything that people believed about [[Odin]] and his rule was all lies that were made into a fresco. Hela used her [[Necroswords]] to completely destroy the fresco and reveal a different one which had been hidden underneath that depicted Hela and Odin [[Subjugation of the Nine Realms|Building their Empire]] through destruction and war. [[File:SkurgeExploringOdinsVault.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge is taken down inside of [[Odin's Vault]]]] The two then went into [[Odin's Vault]] where Skurge admired the great treasures that Odin kept there, only to witness Hela casually pushing over the [[Infinity Gauntlet]] and declaring it to be a fake, noting that most of the items in the Vault were fakes. Skurge then watched on as Hela had called the [[Casket of Ancient Winters]] weak and the [[Crown of Black Fire]] smaller than she had expected it to be, only showing small interest in the [[Tesseract]] which she claimed was not bad. [[File:SkurgeWitnessesBerserkersRebirth.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge watches as [[Berserkers]] are resurrected]] Skurge watched on as Hela found what she had been looking for as she showed him the [[Eternal Flame]], taking the flame into her hand. Hela then proceeded to smash through the floor and found scores of dead [[Einherjar]] buried under the Vault. Skurge then stayed in the Vault and watched as Hela then used the true power of the Eternal Flame to revive her [[Berserkers]] and [[Fenris]], giving her a terrifying army of the undead which Skurge was now forced to serve alongside. [[File:SkurgeExplainingHisBackstory.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge tells [[Hela]] of his desire to prove himself]] Having wiped out [[Asgard]]'s defense and resurrected her own army, Hela then took over the throne as Queen of Asgard. During this time, Skurge informed her of a bunch of [[Asgardians]] were trying to knock down the doors and overthrow Hela. Upon sending her Berserkers to deal with them, Hela then asked Skurge to tell her about himself. He started talking about his family history, but Hela immediately stopped him, explaining that she wanted to know about his ambition. [[File:The Executioner 2.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Hela]] appoints Skurge as her own Executioner]] Skurge then told her that he only ever wanted the chance to prove himself as a warrior. Hearing this, Hela told Skurge of how previous Kings of Asgard had used personal Executions, with Hela herself having been [[Odin]]'s Executioner. Hela then decided to make Skurge her own personal executioner, telling this that this would be a great honor and she made him a [[Bloodaxe]] to become his new weapon of choice, so the two could then finally begin their conquest. [[File:Hela and Skurge.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge and [[Hela]] discover the [[Hofund]] gone]] They then went towards [[Himinbjorg]] with the intention of going through the [[Bifrost Bridge]] and beginning their conquest of all the other [[Nine Realms]] so Hela could become all powerful. However, when they arrived, they discovered that the [[Hofund]] was gone, having seemingly been taken by [[Heimdall]], meaning that they now could not proceed with the plan to take over the realms. Hela had then ordered Skurge to gather the Asgardians who were reeling against her.{{Ref|TR}} ====Threatening Asgard==== [[File:The Executioner.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge as the new Executioner of [[Asgard]]]] {{Quote|Some [[Heimdall|misguided soul]] has stolen the [[Bifrost Bridge|Bifrost]] [[Hofund|Sword]]. Tell us where it is or there will be consequences. Bad ones. Well?"
"You... Well? Executioner?|Skurge and [[Hela]]|Thor: Ragnarok}} Skurge gathered the [[Asgardians]] who had been rebelling against their new Queen and then demanded that somebody finally confess to the location of [[Hofund]], otherwise, there would have to be consequences, indicating towards [[Fenris]]. When no one in the entire crowd had spoken up, [[Hela]] then singled out one of them and ordered Skurge to execute her in front of everybody, which he felt uneasy about as he did not want to kill anyone, let alone an innocent woman. [[File:Skurge-SadFace-PreExecution.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge is told to execute an innocent woman]] Skurge was at first reluctant to do as he had been instructed by Hela, but knew to continue protecting himself he had to kill the innocent woman regardless of his personal feelings. While the horrified crowd looked on helplessly, Skurge raised up his [[Bloodaxe]] and prepared to execute the innocent woman. Fortunately, someone in the crowd had finally spoken out, revealing the true location of the sword, stopping Skurge from killing an innocent woman as Hela had wanted. [[File:Thor Ragnarok 134.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge and [[Hela]] locate the hidden [[Asgardians]]]] The two then made their way to a mountain that [[Heimdall]] was hiding all of the other [[Asgardians]] in who also challenged Hela's claim to the throne of [[Asgard]]. As Skurge watched from the sidelines, still armed with his axe in case there was any resistance, Hela proceeded to use all her [[Necroswords]] to break down the entrance, causing the large doorway to fall before them creating a bridge for Skurge and Hela to cross with ready to confront Heimdall and all the others. [[File:SkurgeHela-FindingHideout.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge and [[Hela]] overhear [[Thor]] calling to them]] The two then entered to find everyone had escaped and were already heading towards the [[Bifrost Bridge]]. Before they could investigate any further, however, suddenly, they heard clanging sounds coming from the [[Royal Palace of Valaskjalf]] where [[Thor]] was banging [[Gungnir]] on the floor in order to call to Hela. While Hela then approached Thor to answer the challenge, Skurge went onto the [[Rainbow Bridge]], knowing that soon Heimdall would attempt to transport with all the Asgardians.{{Ref|TR}} ====[[Battle for Asgard]]==== [[File:SkurgeThreateningHeimdall.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge leading the entire army of [[Berserkers]]]] {{Quote|Heimdall! The [[Hofund|sword]]!|Skurge to [[Heimdall]]|Thor: Ragnarok}} Skurge was then able to trap [[Heimdall]] and all of the rest of the still escaping [[Asgardians]] on the [[Rainbow Bridge]] as they were trying to get to freedom. While [[Fenris]] stopped them from reaching [[Himinbjorg]], Skurge then sent in the army of [[Berserkers]] to charge them, in hopes to retrieve the [[Hofund]] from him to continue [[Hela]]'s conquest. While all the Berserkers fought the Asgardians, Skurge did not actually fight as he only sided with Hela only to survive and not kill his people. [[File:SkurgePreparesToEscape.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge decides to escape from [[Hela]]'s forces]] While the fight had initially gone Skurge and Hela's way as the armies of the Berserkers soon overwhelmed the Asgardians, when [[Loki]] and the [[Sakaaran Rebellion]] had arrived, they offered up valuable backup before Thor returned and destroyed the army. While the Asgardians were being transported onto their ship for freedom, Skurge decided to put down his [[Bloodaxe]] and before making his escape, knowing he would not survive if he continued to side with Hela's armies. [[File:Skurge-EscapingAsgard.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge hides away with the other [[Asgardians]]]] Attempting to get away without being seen, Skurge then disguised himself by putting on a large cloak and hood to hide his armor and head tattoos,{{Ref|TR}} fighting some Berserkers{{Ref|AIWTHJ}} before boarding the ''[[Statesman]]'', continuing his cowardliness to only do what helped him. Skurge then watched on as more of the Asgardians got on board, with several women and children sitting beside him while Thor and Heimdall, along with the help of [[Korg]] and [[Miek]] continued keeping Berserkers at bay.{{Ref|TR}} ====Saving the Asgardians==== [[File:Thor Ragnarok 72.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge makes his final stand for [[Asgard]]]] {{Quote|For [[Asgard]]!|Skurge|Thor: Ragnarok}} Just as the ''[[Statesman]]'' had begun to take off, [[Hela]] managed to stop it by using her [[Necroswords]] to create a large spike that anchored it to the [[Rainbow Bridge]]. As the [[Berserkers]] then began to climb up and eliminate the guards on the ship, making their way towards the innocent women and children. Unwilling to allow this to happen, Skurge revealed himself, using [[Des and Troy]] to destroy the incoming enemies, but knowing they would not stop coming, he jumped out, sacrificing himself. [[File:Executioner Final Stand.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge defeats [[Hela]]'s army of [[Berserkers]]]] As he leaped from the ''Statesmen'', Skurge's shots from Des and Troy destroyed Hela's spike, allowing the ship to be freed. Trapped on the Bridge, Skurge looked up at the departing ship, accepting his fate, but knowing that he was now finally doing the right thing in keeping the [[Asgardians]] safe from harm. He then began to mow down the horde of Berserkers on the Bridge with his rifles with great ease as their bodies shattered from the bullets. [[File:Executioner Death.png|{{AltFloat}}|Skurge is finally killed by [[Hela]]'s necrosword]] Hela noticed Skurge's change insides and was actually intrigued by watching him fight: However, this was then redundant when he ran out of bullets and slowly was overcome with Berserkers. Skurge then used his rifles as clubs to defeat the army, which he then managed to destroy all of them before he called out to Hela. Just as Skurge then came face-to-face with Hela, she responded by unhesitatingly killing Skurge by launching a Necrosword straight through his chest, through his armor.{{Ref|TR}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Asgardian Physiology''': As an [[Asgardian]], Skurge possessed various superhuman attributes. Most notably, strength, speed, and longevity. **'''Superhuman Strength''': Skurge was able to easily wield [[Hofund]] to activate the [[Bifrost Bridge]] for Thor's return from defeating [[Surtur]] and wielded two M16 assault rifles, firing them akimbo with ease. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Skurge was able to withstand many blows while in battle. **'''Superhuman Speed''': When Thor returned from defeating Surtur, Skurge was able to run all the way across the [[Bifrost Bridge]] and to the center of [[Asgard]] in mere minutes. **'''Superhuman Agility''': [[File:Executioner-vs-Berserkers3.jpg|thumb|250px|Skurge leaping onto the [[Rainbow Bridge]]]] Skurge was very agile, being able to battle alongside [[Thor]] and the [[Einherjar]]. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Skurge was able to withstand fatigue very well. However, running all the way across the [[Rainbow Bridge]] tired him out. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Along with his agility, Skurge possessed great reflexes as well. However, he couldn't dodge [[Hela]]'s [[Necroswords]] that ultimately killed him. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Skurge could heal his wounds very quickly which allowed him to return to his battles after getting hurt, however, he was instantly killed when Hela's Necrosword penetrated right through his heart. **'''Longevity''': Skurge's lifespan was tremendously long. However, he could eventually die from old age if he didn't die in battle against Hela. ===Abilities=== *'''Combatant''': Skurge had proficient combat skills, for he survived the [[Battle of Vanaheim]] and could take down several [[Berserkers]] before being killed by [[Hela]]. *'''Marksman''': [[File:Executioner-vs-Berserkers4.jpg|thumb|250px|Skurge shooting at [[Berserkers]]]] Skurge could easily gun down [[Hela]]'s [[Berserkers]] with a pair of assault rifles. He was able to land his shots even when falling through the air. *'''Bilingualism''': Skurge was fluent in his native Asgardian, as well as English. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== {{Quote|I'm particularly fond of these. I pulled 'em out of a place in [[Midgard]] called [[Texas|Tex-as]]. I even named them. [[Des and Troy]]. You see, when you put them together... they destroy.|Skurge|Thor: Ragnarok}} [[File:Executioner-vs-Berserkers2.jpg|thumb|250px|Skurge shooting [[Des and Troy]] at [[Berserkers]]]] *'''[[Des and Troy]]''': Skurge wielded a pair of M16A1 assault rifles that he retrieved in [[Texas]], weapons traditionally adopted for [[Humans]]. He used these rifles to attack the Berserkers that went abroad the ship and threatened to attack the Asgardians. *'''[[Hofund]]''': When Skurge was appointed the Guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge|Bifrost]], he was given Hofund which allowed him the ability to open the Bifrost Bridge. Heimdall eventually regained Hofund after Skurge was deprived of the role. *'''[[Bloodaxe]]''': After [[Hela]]'s return to [[Asgard]], she made Skurge her Executioner and crafted the Bloodaxe with her powers and gave it to him. The Bloodaxe was later thrown away when Skurge decided to abandon his duty as the Executioner and blend in with the crowd to leave Asgard. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Asgardian Armor]]''': Skurge started to use an expensive armor forged by [[Dwarves]] once he was appointed guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]]. The armor is made of underlying interlocking [[Uru]] plates, under heavier plates. The armor is also adorned with his personal sigil on the belt, accented with three blue gems.{{Ref|MSVD}} Despite its Uru plates, however, it proved incapable of protecting Skurge from Hela's [[Necroswords|Necrosword]]. ===Skurge's Stuff=== {{Quote|Behold! My stuff.|Skurge|Thor: Ragnarok}} [[File:Skurge's Stuff.png|thumb|250px|Skurge shows off his personal collection]] During his time as Guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]], Skurge took the opportunity to transport anywhere to steal items and add them to his collection, which he used to impress women. *'''{{WPS|Shake Weight}}''': Skurge kept a Shake Weight, and used it while in company with several [[Asgardian]] women. *'''{{WPS|Vespa}}''': Skurge brought a motor-powered scooter manufactured from [[Earth]]. The appearance of this vehicle is a Vespa brand that is evolved from a single model motor scooter. *'''[[Chitauri Gun]]''': Skurge kept a Chitauri Gun in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''Kegs of Ale''': Skurge kept several kegs of ale in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''Vanir Pole-Axe''': Skurge kept a Vanir pole-axe in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''White Helmet''': Skurge kept a white helmet in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''Jotunheim's Jade Formation''': Skurge kept a Jotunheim's Jade Formation in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''Alfheim's Wooden Staff''': Skurge kept an Alfheim's Wooden Staff in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''Midgard's Missile Launcher''': Skurge kept a Midgardian Missile Launcher in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. *'''Midgardian Wakizashi''': Skurge kept a Midgardian Wakizashi in his collection to impress several Asgardian women. ==Facilities== *'''[[Himinbjorg]]''': While Heimdall was banished by Loki (disguised as Odin), Skurge took over Himinbjorg with Heimdall's sword Hofund to open the Bifrost. ==Relationships== {{col-begin}}{{col-2}} ===Family=== *Father † *Mother † ===Allies=== *[[Asgardian Royal Family]] **[[Odin]] † - Former King **[[Frigga]] † - Former Queen **[[Thor]] - Temporary Enemy **[[Loki]] † - Former King *[[Einherjar]] **[[Tyr]] - Former Leader *[[Sif]] *[[Warriors Three]] **[[Volstagg]] † **[[Fandral]] † **[[Hogun]] † *[[Heimdall]] † - Predecessor and Temporary Enemy *[[Revengers]] - Former Enemies **[[Valkyrie|Brunnhilde/Valkyrie]] **[[Hulk|Bruce Banner/Hulk]] *[[Sakaaran Rebellion]] - Former Enemies **[[Korg]] **[[Miek]] **[[Biff (Rebellion)|Biff]] **[[Tasba]] **[[Roscoe]] † **[[Hajo]] † {{col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Marauders]] *[[Fire Dragon]] † - Victim *[[Hela]] † - Former Queen and Killer *[[Fenris]] † - Former Ally *[[Berserkers]] † - Former Allies {{col-end}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Skurge fell in love with [[Enchantress|Amora the Enchantress]], who began to use him as a bodyguard and in her various schemes to seduce [[Thor]], conquer [[Asgard]] and help [[Loki]]. *Skurge's death is a direct reference to his last stand from "Thor #362" (December 1985), where he fended off Hela's army with a pair of {{WPS|M16A1|M16A1 assault rifles}}, at the cost of his life, to save the Asgardians from [[Berserkers]]. *Skurge thinks tattoos make him look attractive.{{Ref|MSCE}} ==Behind the Scenes== *On Skurge aligning with [[Hela]], [[Karl Urban]] described it as ''"a working relationship"'', and said, ''"I think he makes a deal with the devil. And that's when the rubber meets the road... He’s her henchman. He does the dirty jobs. And that sort of is something he has to – it plays on his conscience. It crossed a certain moral line."'' [http://www.comingsoon.net/movies/features/872987-comic-con-tessa-thompson-karl-urban-and-taiki-waititi-on-thor#/slide/1 Tessa Thompson, Karl Urban and Taiki Waititi on Thor] *[[Karl Urban]] states that: ''"Skurge quickly realizes that he either has to join [[Hela|her]] or die. He's not the sharpest tool in the shed, but he's bright enough to make the right choice for him. He's a survivalist."''[http://www.latimes.com/entertainment/herocomplex/la-ca-mn-sneaks-thor-newcomers-20170903-htmlstory.html 'Thor: Ragnarok' cast introduces the newest members of the Marvel Cinematic Universe] *[[Ryan Green]] was a stunt double for [[Karl Urban]] in the role of Skurge. ==References== {{Gallery|Executioner/Gallery}} {{Wq|Executioner}} {{Reflist}} {{Succession box |before = [[Heimdall]] |title = Guardian of the [[Bifrost Bridge]] |current = Skurge |succeeded = [[Volstagg]] |years = [[2017]] |after = -}} {{Succession box |before = [[Hela]] |title = Executioner of Asgard |current = Skurge |succeeded = - |years = [[2017]] |after = -}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP|Executioner (comics)|Executioner}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/skurge|Executioner}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Thor: Ragnarok Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Asgardians]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Bald Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Einherjar Members]] [[Category:High Body Count]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]] [[Category:Characters Killed by Hela]]
